project.name: media-nest
project.description: MediaNest is an Android-first, offline-centric media library application designed for personal media consumption and organization. It allows you to download YouTube videos and audio locally, organize them into folders, maintain playback history, and optionally sync your metadata across devices. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.summary: Performs the backup export to a ZipOutputStream.

nodes.summary: Repository class containing the export methods.

nodes.summary: Implements restore procedures, reading JSON database snapshots and media assets from a backup ZIP archive and importing them back into Room databases.

nodes.summary: Main Application class for MediaNest, initializing dependency injection with Hilt, NewPipe extractor, notification channels, and scheduling startup tasks like background backups and subscription checks.

nodes.name: onCreate

nodes.name: LibraryRepair.kt
nodes.summary: Provides diagnostic and repair utilities to scan media directories on disk, match files with the Room database, fix broken paths, and prune orphaned entries.

nodes.name: LibraryRepair
nodes.summary: Database repair service executing IO disk scanning and metadata validation to resolve consistency issues between downloaded files and database tables.

nodes.name: repair
nodes.summary: Executes the repair operation by scanning directories, resolving path changes, clearing missing video paths, and cleaning orphan downloads.

nodes.name: DevicePreferences.kt
nodes.summary: Manages server settings, device synchronization metadata, and general UI library view preferences using Jetpack Preferences DataStore.

nodes.name: DownloadPreferences.kt
nodes.summary: Manages download-related configurations such as maximum concurrent downloads, download directory paths, and auto-backup interval schedules using Preferences DataStore.

nodes.name: DownloadPreferences
nodes.summary: Accesses and modifies settings for max concurrent files, download target directory, and scheduled auto-backup timers through DataStore Flows.

nodes.name: setAutoBackupIntervalHours
nodes.summary: Updates the backup frequency in hours and resets or starts the scheduled timestamp in the DataStore preferences.

nodes.name: SyncRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Handles HTTP requests for client registration and database synchronization push/pull actions with the remote server using OkHttpClient.

nodes.name: SyncRepository
nodes.summary: Repository containing Hilt-injected OkHttpClient, Kotlinx JSON serializer, and methods to register a client and push/pull library mutations.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Registers the current device with the remote server by posting its device name and returning the generated server credentials.

nodes.name: pushChanges
nodes.summary: Pushes local watch history and playlist modifications to the remote synchronization endpoint via HTTP POST.

nodes.name: pullChanges
nodes.summary: Pulls remote library changes that happened after a given sync version using an HTTP GET query.

nodes.name: DownloadModule.kt
nodes.summary: Dagger Hilt module providing a singleton instance of DownloadPreferences for dependency injection across the codebase.

nodes.name: DownloadModule
nodes.summary: Provides the application-wide single instance of DownloadPreferences.

nodes.name: PreferencesModule.kt
nodes.summary: Dagger Hilt module providing a singleton instance of DevicePreferences for dependency injection across the codebase.

nodes.name: PreferencesModule
nodes.summary: Provides the application-wide single instance of DevicePreferences.

nodes.name: AudioExtractor.kt
nodes.summary: Extracts audio tracks from video files by first attempting native demuxing to M4A, falling back to FFmpeg transcoder for MP3 conversion if needed.

nodes.name: AudioExtractor
nodes.summary: Singleton class offering methods to extract high-quality audio files from downloaded videos nimbly.

nodes.name: extractAudio
nodes.summary: Coordinates audio extraction, trying the faster native demuxing first and resorting to FFmpeg-based MP3 conversion on failure.

nodes.name: extractAudioNatively
nodes.summary: Performs native extraction of AAC streams from video containers and muxes them into M4A files using MediaExtractor and MediaMuxer.

nodes.name: SettingsScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Main settings screen UI built in Jetpack Compose, enabling configuration of download paths, backup management, database repairs, server sync, and app updates.

nodes.name: SettingsScreen
nodes.summary: The main composable function laying out settings categories, fields, logs, backup cards, update checks, and initiating dialog actions.

nodes.name: getPathFromUri
nodes.summary: Helper utility that converts a SAF content Uri back to a raw absolute file path on Android.

nodes.name: ExportImportViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: ViewModel backing the settings screen, coordinating storage migrations, local and remote backup imports/exports, server synchronization triggers, and client updating via GitHub API.

nodes.name: ExportImportViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel exposing Flows for configuration states and executing library repair, SAF zip import/export, and folder migration.

nodes.name: startDownloadFolderMigration
nodes.summary: Asynchronously migrates media files from an old location to a new download folder path on disk, updates database references, and updates settings.

nodes.name: inspectImportFile
nodes.summary: Inspects a potential zip backup file via SAF, verifying if it contains valid database entries and media files before restoring.

nodes.name: restoreFromFile
nodes.summary: Restores database and optional media assets from a user-supplied zip file using RestoreRepository.

nodes.name: exportToFile
nodes.summary: Triggers zip backup export to a SAF destination stream and updates operation progress states.

nodes.name: repairLibrary
nodes.summary: Triggers library diagnostic and cleanup logic via LibraryRepair and updates the UI state with the result details.

nodes.name: checkForUpdates
nodes.summary: Queries GitHub releases to determine if a newer APK version is available, returning release details.

nodes.name: downloadAndInstallUpdate
nodes.summary: Downloads the latest update APK file from GitHub assets with progress updates.

nodes.name: installApk
nodes.summary: Requests Android package installer activity to open and install the downloaded update APK.

nodes.name: loadLocalBackups
nodes.summary: Reads metadata of backup zip files present in the local backup folder to list them in the settings screen.

nodes.name: createLocalBackup
nodes.summary: Creates an automated metadata backup in the local storage, maintaining a maximum limit of recent files.

nodes.name: restoreFromLocalBackup
nodes.summary: Restores library states using a locally selected backup zip file.

nodes.name: redownloadMissingFiles
nodes.summary: Scans for database entries with missing local files and schedules DownloadService tasks to re-download them.

nodes.name: AutoBackupWorker.kt
nodes.summary: WorkManager CoroutineWorker that schedules automated, periodic backups of database metadata to a zip archive on local disk.

nodes.name: AutoBackupWorker
nodes.summary: Dagger-assisted worker executing the background auto-backup routine and maintaining local retention policy limits.

nodes.name: doWork
nodes.summary: Executes the backup operation by creating a timestamped zip metadata archive and deleting older backups to maintain a limit of 3 files.

nodes.name: WorkScheduler.kt
nodes.summary: Helper object providing centralized methods to schedule, cancel, and update periodic Android WorkManager background tasks.

nodes.name: WorkScheduler
nodes.summary: Entry scheduler containing utility methods to enqueue periodic background subscription checking, remote server sync, and auto-backups.

nodes.name: scheduleSubscriptionCheck
nodes.summary: Schedules a unique periodic work request for SubscriptionWorker to check channel updates every 6 hours under network availability.

nodes.name: scheduleSync
nodes.summary: Schedules a unique periodic work request for SyncWorker to sync data with the server at a configurable interval.

nodes.name: scheduleAutoBackup
nodes.summary: Schedules a unique periodic work request for AutoBackupWorker to run metadata backup at a configurable interval.

nodes.name: DownloaderProvider
nodes.summary: Downloader factory that constructs HTTP clients with customized connection properties and headers.

nodes.name: getDownloader

nodes.name: YouTubeExtractor.kt

nodes.name: YouTubeExtractor
nodes.summary: Service class responsible for resolving video, playlist, and channel details from URLs using NewPipe Extractor parsing methods.

nodes.name: extractVideo
nodes.summary: Extracts metadata and streaming endpoints for a YouTube video URL.

nodes.name: extractPlaylist
nodes.summary: Extracts list of video metadata for a YouTube playlist URL.

nodes.name: stripChannelTab
nodes.summary: Removes tab suffixes from channel URLs to normalize them.

nodes.name: sanitizeChannelUrl
nodes.summary: Sanitizes YouTube channel URLs to a standard format suitable for extraction.

nodes.name: extractChannel
nodes.summary: Extracts channel metadata and its uploaded videos from a channel URL.

nodes.name: HomeViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: ViewModel that manages home screen UI states, URL query submissions, extraction history, and subscription actions.

nodes.name: HomeViewModel
nodes.summary: ViewModel implementation holding state and exposing methods for user interactions on the Home screen.

nodes.name: HomeUiState
nodes.summary: Sealed interface representing the UI state of the Home screen (Idle, Loading, Success, Error).

nodes.name: onUrlSubmitted
nodes.summary: Handles user URL submissions, extracting video/playlist/channel metadata and persisting logs and metadata in the background.

nodes.name: saveLinkToHistory

nodes.name: ExampleUnitTest.kt

nodes.summary: Unit test class containing test methods for NewPipe-based media extractors.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Defines global server configurations including the SQLite database file path and master API key.

nodes.name: database.py
nodes.summary: Manages SQLite connection lifecycle using thread-local caching and defines the database schema for devices and sync changes.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application entry point setting up routes, CORS middleware, and lifespan database initialization.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty Python package initializer for the routes module.

nodes.name: devices.py
nodes.summary: Defines router endpoints for registering new devices and deleting existing devices along with their changes.

nodes.name: health.py
nodes.summary: Defines a basic health check router endpoint returning the status of the sync server.

nodes.name: sync.py
nodes.summary: Implements the synchronization push and pull router endpoints for saving and retrieving incremental client database changes.

nodes.name: schemas.py
nodes.summary: Defines Pydantic models for API request validation and response serialization.

nodes.name: get_connection
nodes.summary: Creates or returns a thread-local SQLite connection with Row factory, WAL mode, and foreign keys enabled.

nodes.name: init_db
nodes.summary: Initializes the database schema by creating 'devices' and 'changes' tables, adding indices, and setting up an auto-versioning trigger.

nodes.name: lifespan
nodes.summary: Context manager handling server startup by ensuring the database directory exists and initializing database tables.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Registers a new device with generated device ID and API key, validating against the master key.

nodes.name: delete_device
nodes.summary: Deletes a device and all of its associated changes from the database, verifying the API key.

nodes.name: verify_device
nodes.summary: Checks if the device ID matches the provided API key in the database.

nodes.name: push
nodes.summary: Saves device changes to the 'changes' database table in an exclusive transaction and updates the last sync timestamp.

nodes.name: pull
nodes.summary: Retrieves changes from other devices since a specific sequence version inside a transaction, updating the sync timestamp.

nodes.name: sync-server/docker-compose.yml

nodes.name: uv
nodes.summary: Docker container stage based on Astral uv image, exposing port 8000 and running the uvicorn server.

layers.name: Android Presentation & UI Layer
layers.description: Android user interface and presentation components, including Jetpack Compose screens, themes, ViewModels, and drawable/layout resources.

layers.name: Android Business Logic & Services
layers.description: Android core logic, repository implementations, dependency injection components, media player integrations, data synchronizers, and background workers.

layers.name: Android Local Data Access
layers.description: Android local persistence components, SQLite database setups, Room ORM entities, DAOs, type converters, and schema migration history.

layers.name: Sync Server API Routing
layers.description: FastAPI synchronization server web routes, API endpoint definitions, and application bootloader entry points.

layers.name: Sync Server Services & Core Data
layers.description: Sync server database connection logic, SQLAlchemy ORM models, Pydantic data serialization schemas, and helper services.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Deployment
layers.description: Docker orchestration configurations, Dockerfiles, Linux deployment configurations, Systemd service profiles, and release scripts.

layers.name: Project & Build Configuration
layers.description: Gradle project builds, module configurations, Android Manifest files, environment templates, Proguard rules, and tool preferences.

layers.name: Documentation & Planning
layers.description: Project guidelines, architecture maps, implementation task checklists, review documents, and general system references.

layers.name: Testing & Verification
layers.description: Android unit tests, instrumented integration tests, and general verification scripts.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Welcome to MediaNest! This Android-first, offline-centric application is designed for personal media consumption and organization. It supports offline-first local storage, YouTube downloading via NewPipeExtractor, ExoPlayer media playback, local catalog organization, and optional VPS synchronization using a FastAPI server. In this step, we read the project README to get a high-level understanding of features and goals.

tour.title: Build & Dependency Management
tour.description: Before diving into code, let's explore the project structure and build configurations. The root 'build.gradle.kts' and app 'build.gradle.kts' handle compilation, plugin injection, and dependency organization for the Android side using Version Catalogs ('libs.versions.toml'). On the server side, 'pyproject.toml' manages FastAPI python dependencies and packaging.

tour.title: Application Entry Points
tour.description: Every application has its launching pad. For the Android application, 'MediaNestApp' extends 'Application' and initializes Hilt dependency injection, while 'MainActivity' sets up the Compose UI content and handles system permissions. On the backend, 'sync-server/app/main.py' bootstraps the FastAPI application and includes API routers.

tour.title: Local Database Architecture
tour.description: MediaNest operates on an offline-first database. The Room database 'AppDatabase' lists the schema entities and exposes their DAOs. 'VideoEntity' defines local media records (such as titles, urls, watch history, and download state), while 'VideoDao' defines SQLite queries for fetching, updating, and deleting video items.

tour.title: Media Extraction
tour.description: One of MediaNest's core features is extraction of video and playlist metadata from YouTube. 'YouTubeExtractor' uses NewPipeExtractor to retrieve video streams and format information, while 'DownloaderProvider' handles custom HTTP fetching and cookies setup required for extracting YouTube streams.

tour.title: Downloader Service & Local Files
tour.description: After video extraction, downloading is executed. 'DownloadService' manages background download tasks, fetching stream chunks, displaying notifications, and handling network state changes. 'DownloadRepository' interfaces with the database to track download progress and state.

tour.title: ExoPlayer Playback Integration
tour.description: MediaNest integrates Google's ExoPlayer via a background 'PlaybackService' to ensure audio/video playback can continue playing when the app is in background. The UI binds to the media player state via 'PlayerViewModel' and displays controls, video outputs, and settings on 'PlayerScreen'.

tour.title: Database Backups & Restore
tour.description: To secure offline user data, MediaNest has local backup features. 'BackupRepository' serializes preference databases and local Room records into a ZIP archive, while 'RestoreRepository' unpacks and deserializes those ZIP files back into the Room database, handling migration checks and file repair.

tour.title: FastAPI & Remote Data Synchronization
tour.description: MediaNest optionally synchronizes data across devices. On Android, 'SyncManager' coordinates database synchronization with the remote backend, triggered periodically in the background by WorkManager's 'SyncWorker'. On the server side, FastAPI endpoint 'routes/sync.py' handles incoming synchronization payloads, conflict resolution, and saves them to a PostgreSQL or SQLite backend.

tour.title: Server Deployment & Infrastructure
tour.description: Finally, let's look at the infrastructure that runs the synchronization server. The FastAPI backend is dockerized via 'sync-server/Dockerfile' using 'uv' (a fast Python package manager and runner). The complete container topology, including databases and env configuration, is orchestrated with 'sync-server/docker-compose.yml'.
